div1.entryFree: Collier, a digger or seller of coals: since her time are --s counted bright, LLL IV, 3, 267. 'tis not for gravity to play at cherry-pit with Satan: hang him, foul c.! Tw. III, 4, 130 (alluding to the proverb: like will to like, as the devil with the collier). we'll not carry coals; no, for then we should be --s, Rom. I, 1, 3.
